summary.intent: Ensure the UI displays checkpoint summaries from the most recent session, and migrate checkpoint fetching from the old V1 API to the new V2 API.
summary.outcome: Both list and detail endpoints now sort sessions by createdAt DESC before selecting a summary, and the UI fetches checkpoints via a new GET /api/v2/sessions/:id/checkpoints endpoint instead of the old V1 endpoint.

repo: V2_SESSION_INCLUDE is a reusable Prisma include object used in findMany calls in src/server.ts; orderBy must be a top-level findMany argument, not nested inside include.
SessionDetail.tsx merges checkpoint events into the timeline by sorting checkpoints by createdAt ascending and doing a linear merge-insert based on timestamp comparison.
fetchSessionCheckpoints previously called the V1 /api/sessions/:id/checkpoints endpoint backed by old Checkpoint/CheckpointSession tables; now replaced with a V2 endpoint.

code.finding: Added GET /api/v2/sessions/:id/checkpoints endpoint returning checkpoints sorted by createdAt ascending.
Updated fetchSessionCheckpoints to call /api/v2/sessions/:id/checkpoints instead of the V1 endpoint.

Checkpoint merge logic sorts by createdAt ascending and inserts checkpoints before events whose timestamp is >= checkpoint.createdAt; null createdAt falls back to empty string, placing those checkpoints first.

workflow: Prisma orderBy must be at the findMany level, not inside include blocks — a quick mistake to make when refactoring includes.
After backend changes, curl-testing the new endpoint directly before wiring up the client is an effective verification step.

friction: Initial edit incorrectly placed orderBy inside the include block rather than as a top-level findMany argument, requiring a follow-up fix.
open_items: If session-timeline checkpoints are ever migrated fully to V2, the fetchSessionCheckpoints call and merge logic in SessionDetail.tsx would need to be revisited to ensure compatibility.
The old V1 /api/sessions/:id/checkpoints endpoint still exists in the server but is no longer called by the UI — it could be removed as cleanup.
